items.text:
Subject: Welcome to the Brightwood Makerspace!

Hi Maya,

Welcome to the Brightwood Makerspace on the lower floor of Kessler Hall! We're thrilled you joined, and we want to help you start building right away.

Your membership gives you access to the laser cutters, 3D printers, and woodworking benches whenever the space is open, Monday through Saturday, 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM. Before you use any powered tool, though, you must finish a short safety orientation. Sessions run every Tuesday at 4:00 PM in Room 12, and you simply sign up on the member portal.

Materials like basic wood and filament are free, but specialty supplies are sold at our front desk. For example, a sheet of acrylic costs $8, and laser time beyond your monthly hour runs $0.50 per minute. We accept campus cards only, so please load yours before visiting.

Feel free to stop by anytime with questions. We can't wait to see what you make.

Thanks,
Reuben Park
Member Coordinator, Brightwood Makerspace

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Maya do before using a powered tool?

options.A: Complete a safety orientation held in Room 12.
options.B: Purchase a sheet of acrylic at the front desk.
options.C: Reserve a laser cutter through the member portal.
options.D: Load extra laser minutes onto her campus card.

questions.explanation: The email states that before using any powered tool, members must finish a short safety orientation, with sessions running Tuesdays at 4:00 PM in Room 12. The other options describe real items from the text placed in the wrong context.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about paying for supplies at the makerspace?

options.A: It is waived entirely for new members during their first month.
options.B: It requires advance approval from the member coordinator each time.
options.C: It cannot be done with cash or an ordinary credit card.
options.D: It includes a discount applied automatically at the front desk.

questions.explanation: The email notes that the makerspace accepts campus cards only and asks members to load theirs before visiting, implying cash and regular credit cards are not accepted.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To announce new operating hours for the makerspace this term.
options.B: To remind members that specialty supplies must be purchased.
options.C: To request that new members sign up for a Tuesday session.
options.D: To welcome a new member and explain how to get started.

questions.explanation: The email greets a new member and then walks through access hours, the required orientation, and how supplies are paid for, all to help her begin using the space. The other options each capture only a single detail.

items.text:
Subject: Your Flu Shot Appointment Is Confirmed

Dear Mr. Okafor,

This message confirms your flu shot appointment at the Hartley Student Health Clinic. You are scheduled for Thursday, October 9, at 2:30 PM in the Immunization Room on the second floor of Hartley Hall.

Please arrive about ten minutes early to check in at the front desk, and bring your student ID card and your insurance information. The shot itself is free for enrolled students, but a $15 service charge applies if you miss the appointment without canceling at least 24 hours ahead. You can cancel or reschedule through the health portal at any time.

We recommend wearing a short-sleeved shirt so the nurse can reach your upper arm easily. After the shot, you'll wait in the lobby for fifteen minutes so staff can make sure you feel fine before you leave.

If you have a fever or feel unwell on the day, please reschedule rather than coming in.

Best regards,
Nurse Patricia Lund
Hartley Student Health Clinic

format_metadata.subject: Your Flu Shot Appointment Is Confirmed

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Mr. Okafor bring to his appointment?

options.A: A short-sleeved shirt and a completed health form.
options.B: His student ID card and his insurance information.
options.C: A printed confirmation from the health portal page.
options.D: A payment of the fifteen-dollar service charge.

questions.explanation: The email asks Mr. Okafor to bring his student ID card and insurance information. The shirt is a recommendation rather than something to bring, and the other options mix in details placed in the wrong context.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the fifteen-minute lobby wait after the shot?

options.A: It allows the clinic to collect the service charge before leaving.
options.B: It gives students time to fill out their insurance paperwork.
options.C: It lets staff watch for any immediate reaction to the shot.
options.D: It is required only for students who report feeling unwell.

questions.explanation: The email says students wait fifteen minutes so staff can make sure they feel fine before leaving, implying the wait is to monitor for any immediate reaction to the vaccine.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To remind students that flu shots are free this season.
options.B: To confirm an appointment and explain what to expect.
options.C: To warn about the charge for missing a scheduled visit.
options.D: To describe the location of the clinic in Hartley Hall.

questions.explanation: The email confirms the appointment's date, time, and place, then explains arrival, payment, and after-care details so the patient knows what to expect. The other options each restate only one detail.
